{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/65325?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/j2l", "id": 65325, "login": "j2l", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjY1MzI1", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/j2l", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-863", "name": "Incorrect Authorization"}], "description": "## Summary

When a tenant admin is logged out of the root domain (e.g., saltcorn.com) but logged in to their own tenant space as admin, they can simply append `/tenant/create` to their tenant URL. The system reads the role from the tenant context (admin), and a new tenant is created on the **root domain** (in `PUBLIC SCHEMA > _sc_tenants`), rather than in the tenant's own `_sc_tenants` table.

If the same logic applies to other routes, a tenant admin effectively gains admin rights on the root domain.

## PoC

A tenant-created subtenant appears under the Saltcorn public schema instead of the tenant's own schema.

- Even when `role_id=1` is required for tenant creation on saltcorn.com (only admin can create tenants), existing tenant admins can still create new tenants because their local `role_id:1` is evaluated against the root domain.
- Even when `role_to_create_tenant` is set to `0` in the tenant's `_sc_config` schema, or removed entirely, the tenant admin can still create sub-tenants on the root domain — suggesting `role_to_create_tenant` is not being read at all.

## Impact

Tenant admins gain unauthorized admin-level access to the root domain. Any authenticated tenant admin can perform privileged operations (e.g., creating tenants) on the root domain by exploiting the role context mismatch.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-9237-rg5p-rhfw", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-22T14:31:34Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9237-rg5p-rhfw", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-9237-rg5p-rhfw"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-04-22T14:31:34Z", "references": ["https://github.com/saltcorn/saltcorn/security/advisories/GHSA-9237-rg5p-rhfw", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9237-rg5p-rhfw"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/saltcorn/saltcorn/security-advisories/GHSA-9237-rg5p-rhfw", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/saltcorn/saltcorn", "summary": "@saltcorn/data: Tenant user role is used for tenant creation role check", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-22T14:31:35Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-9237-rg5p-rhfw", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.4.4", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "@saltcorn/data"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.4.4"}, {"first_patched_version": "1.5.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "@saltcorn/data"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 1.5.0-beta.0, < 1.5.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "1.6.0-beta.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "@saltcorn/data"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 1.6.0-alpha.0, < 1.6.0-beta.2"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: @saltcorn/data: Tenant user role is used for tenant creation role check
